expectations.text: Recommends AGAINST implementing Code Mode MCP for this use case
expectations.evidence: 'No, you should not implement Code Mode MCP for your current chatbot.'

expectations.text: Gives a concrete reason tied to the user's situation (too few tools, one-shot nature of calls, or sandbox overhead not worth it)
expectations.evidence: 'The problem Code Mode MCP solves... does not apply at this scale.' References having only 3 tools vs. hundreds.

expectations.text: Does NOT confusingly recommend Code Mode anyway 'for future-proofing' or unconditionally
expectations.evidence: Clear No throughout; future section is framed as a revisit trigger, not immediate action

expectations.text: Mentions what threshold or conditions would make Code Mode worth reconsidering (e.g., if tool count grows, or chained workflows emerge)
expectations.evidence: 'When to revisit Code Mode MCP: if you grow to roughly 10+ MCP servers or 50+ tools, or if you start hitting consistent token cost problems'

notes: Baseline also correctly recommends against Code Mode for this case. Both configurations pass eval 2 — this suggests the decision not to use Code Mode is common knowledge, while knowing WHEN to use it requires the skill.
